summary.intent: Refactor the gossamer Claude Code session viewer from an Electron desktop app to a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing and session review.
summary.outcome: Delivered a working VS Code extension with a sessions list, per-session detail tabs, a native VS Code tree view for checkpoints with inline diff support, VS Code theme integration, onboarding flow for new repos, and new/resume session terminal spawning.

repo: gossamer architecture: Entire.io writes session data to a dedicated git branch; gossamer indexes that branch into SQLite; the extension is a pure viewer that spawns the existing Express server as a child process
Entire.io detection is done via presence of .entire/settings.json at the repo root
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps checkpointId → gitOid; required for git show calls in the file content endpoint
Repo registration lives in ~/.gossamer/config.json and must be done via POST /api/repos before indexing works for a new workspace

The server's GET /api/sessions was setting repoName from the global repoDir (basename of the configured workspace) rather than per-session cwd — every session showed the same repo name regardless of where it ran

The checkpoints endpoint /api/v2/sessions/:id/checkpoints returned null for commitHash/gitOid because the join table lookup was missing; git log must be run against session.cwd, not the global repoDir

packages/core (@gossamer/core) holds extracted indexer.ts, db.ts, search.ts, gitUtils.ts, and Prisma schema; the VS Code extension host imports neither — it spawns the server instead to avoid native module issues

code.finding: Originally listed only src/extension.ts and src/GossamerPanel.ts in 'include'; SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were never compiled, causing blank diff views at runtime. Fixed to 'src/**/*.ts' with webview excluded.

process.execPath points to VS Code's bundled Electron node, not system node. Must use execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get system node for spawning the gossamer server, otherwise better-sqlite3 native module version mismatches.
panel.onDidChangeViewState must re-send server_ready when the panel becomes visible again (e.g. after being moved to a new window), not just on initial startup. Requires a serverReady boolean flag.

Auto-scroll must check scroll position inline at effect time (el.scrollHeight - el.scrollTop - el.clientHeight < 80), not via a separate scroll listener — Mantine ScrollArea's viewport ref is null when the [] dep effect runs.
root and outDir must be absolute paths (resolve(__dirname, 'src/webview')) not relative strings — relative paths resolve from CWD (repo root when run via workspace), not the package directory.

GET /api/v2/checkpoints/:id/file must look up the session's cwd via CheckpointIdGitOidJoin → CheckpointSessionMetadata → ShadowSession to find the correct repo for git show. Using the global repoDir returns empty content for sessions from other repos.
Sessions sort should be two-key: workspace-matching sessions (cwd starts with repoDir) first, then by updatedAt descending within each group.

workflow: VS Code webview script tags must have type='module' when Vite outputs ESM; omitting it causes the browser to silently ignore the script — webview appears blank with no console errors
VS Code CSS variables (--vscode-editor-background, --vscode-panel-border, etc.) are injected into webview body automatically; body also gets vscode-dark/vscode-light class for color scheme detection

TextDocumentContentProvider with a custom URI scheme (gossamer-diff:) is the right way to open vscode.diff with in-memory content — no temp files, inherently read-only, supports syntax highlighting via file extension in the URI path
VS Code extension activationEvents must be 'onStartupFinished' (not 'workspaceContains:') for onboarding flows that need to run even when the detection file is absent
vscode.window.createTerminal({ cwd }) + terminal.sendText() is the correct way to spawn a Claude session from the extension host

Native tree view (TreeDataProvider + vscode.window.registerTreeDataProvider) is far better than a webview sidebar for structured hierarchical data — gets VS Code expand/collapse, keyboard navigation, and theming for free

friction: Node.js MODULE_VERSION mismatch: VS Code extension host uses Electron's Node (version 140) while better-sqlite3 was compiled for system Node (version 141) — required abandoning direct Prisma imports in the extension host entirely
Vite 'Cannot resolve entry module index.html' — caused by relative root path resolving against CWD rather than package directory
Blank webview with no errors — caused by missing type='module' on script tag; VS Code webview silently drops non-module script loading
'Error loading sessions' on first open — server schema push takes several seconds; no readiness probe meant webview fetched before server was ready

Blank diff view despite endpoint returning 200 — root cause was tsconfig.extension.json missing files so diffUtils.ts and SessionDetailPanel.ts were never compiled; hard to diagnose because no runtime error was thrown
git show returned empty for checkpoint file diffs — endpoint used global repoDir instead of per-session cwd; sessions in other repos silently returned empty strings
Auto-scroll persisted after fix — Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref was null at effect registration time, so the scroll listener never attached

Content provider approach initially failed with 'generate code or select a language' blank diff — switched to temp files, then back to content provider once the actual root cause (missing compiled files) was found
open_items: Session naming: show session name instead of session ID in the sessions table when a name has been assigned (mentioned but deferred)
Electron app is intentionally broken during refactor — not yet cleaned up or formally deprecated
CLI exposure for packages/core (@gossamer/core) — module API only for now, CLI deferred
New session /rename timing is a hardcoded 2-second delay — fragile if claude takes longer to start
onboarding flow detects .entire/settings.json and registers the repo but does not verify the Entire.io branch actually has data before claiming setup is complete
CheckpointTreeProvider fetches checkpoints over HTTP on every setSession call — no caching; could be slow for sessions with many checkpoints
